review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience with Minimalics Official.
 
 I joined expecting verified and quality leads, but the leads shared with me turned out to be completely useless and unqualified. Out of the 3 leads I received, two were junk leads that did not meet the promised standards. On the other hand, I payed for 6 leads out of which they shared only 3.
 
 After receiving the poor-quality leads, I emailed the team requesting a replacement, but I never received any response. Even more concerning, the remaining three leads that were supposed to be shared were never provided.
 
 I made multiple phone calls and followed up repeatedly to resolve the issue, but instead of addressing my concerns, they eventually blocked my number. This level of unprofessional behavior and lack of customer support is unacceptable.
 
 Based on my experience, I would not recommend Minimalics Official to anyone looking for reliable lead-sharing services. The leads provided were of poor quality, commitments were not fulfilled, and customer concerns were ignored.

1/5
 
 My experience with Minimalix has been extremely disappointing.
 
 I spent nearly two weeks following their process, providing information, responding to requests, and waiting for results. During this entire period, I did not receive any meaningful leads from my area despite the expectations set when I joined.
 
 After realizing that the service was not delivering what was promised, I requested cancellation and a refund. Since then, I have had to repeatedly follow up for updates. Even after providing all the information they requested, including my bank account details, I continue to receive vague responses stating that the matter is "under investigation."
 
 I was specifically told that I would receive an update within 24 hours. That deadline passed without any meaningful response, refund initiation date, or clear timeline for when my money would be returned.
 
 The most frustrating part is the lack of transparency and accountability. Two weeks were wasted waiting for results that never came, and now I am left chasing my own money.
 
 At this point, it feels as though once a customer has paid the money, the company treats it as if it were a donation rather than a payment for a service. Customers should not have to spend weeks waiting for promised results and then spend additional time chasing a refund.
 
 Customers deserve honest communication, timely support, and a refund process that does not require constant follow-ups.
 
 I hope Minimalix resolves this matter immediately and improves the way it handles customer concerns and refund requests

"Livpace: Where Deadlines Go to Die"
 Hired Livpace for a kitchen makeover. Simple enough, right? WRONG.
 They promised a move-in date of 20th May with the confidence of someone who actually has a plan. Spoiler: they did not have a plan. They did not have teams. They barely had workers.
 What they DID have was an incredible talent for showing up with random daily wage labourers different faces every day, zero briefing, zero tools, maximum confusion. Some days nobody showed up at all. Turns out it's hard to get work done when you're not paying your workers. Revolutionary concept, apparently lost on Livpace.
 45 days later our family is still living inside what can only be described as a very expensive construction site. No apology. No timeline. No accountability. Just vibes and excuses.
 Their coordination strategy seems to be: pick strangers off the street, point them at a kitchen, and hope for the best. It is not working.
 If you're looking for someone to take your money, miss your deadline by months, gaslight you about professionalism, and make your family's daily life genuinely miserable — Livpace is absolutely your company.
 For everyone else — run. Fast. Far. Don't look back.
 P.S. — Still waiting, Livpace. The internet is very patient.

A cautionary account for anyone considering Livspace for their home interior project.
 
 We are 6+ weeks past our original delivery date. Here is what the journey has looked like:
 
 🔴 Three designer changes — each without a proper handover, forcing us to repeat requirements from scratch.
 
 🔴 A laminate selection meeting where our choices were severely restricted and we were told we "had to choose" from what was available — this while my wife attended just one month postpartum.
 
 🔴 An SOD (Scope of Design) document floated to us riddled with errors, clearly not peer-reviewed before client sharing.
 
 🔴 And now — post-production — a full wardrobe unit delivered at the wrong depth (18 inches instead of the standard 580mm we requested repeatedly), with the team retroactively calling it a "storage unit" instead of a wardrobe.
 
 At every milestone, we have raised concerns in writing. At every milestone, we have been met with promises that didn't materialise.
 
 We are not designers — we are clients. It is the service provider's responsibility to communicate deviations, not assume clients will figure it out. The SOD itself says "wardrobe." The group chat messages confirm our requirements.
 
 If you are evaluating Livspace for your home, please do your due diligence. Ask hard questions about designer continuity, escalation protocols, and production accountability before signing.
 
 We still hope for a resolution. But we believe others deserve to know what this process has looked like.
 
 We are still following up but not getting any response and not even sure if Livspace is trying to find a resolution or not.
 
 Giving 1 star because that is the least option I can select they don't even deserve this much.

1. The kitchen installation was delayed by over a month and we did not receive timely updates with regards to completion ETA. Things got in to motion only after I escalated the issue
 2. Almirahs were installed but the handles were not installed until I escalated it which was almost 20 days after the almirah was installed.
 3. Locks in the almirahs have still not been installed and there has been no update regarding the same. That too after there has been such a high severity escalation on this project.
 4. The chimney hole that has been marked by your team has been done right at the bottom of the loft due to which we barely have any space to use in the loft. Ideally it should have been at the top of the loft, had it been up to us we wouldn't have gone ahead with it but since your team guided us we thought it be okay and only realized that there has been a mistake once the chimney pipe went through.
 5. One of the almirahs that has been installed is misaligned to do improper installation. Also the shutters have bent out within 2 months. Your team said it is due to the rise temperature but as far as I know the temp in Noida is only going to rise further, so should we expect this to happen throughout the summers? Even if you replace them as per the warranty agreement, with the temp rising further in coming months will this keep happening!?
 6. The skirting for one of the almirahs was not installed and when we called it out we were told that livspace almirahs do not have skirtings on them. However, the skirting was lying around at the site and was installed right away. Which goes to show your team did not have complete knowledge about the fixtures for this project.
 7. No provisions have been made for lights inside the almirah even though at the time of placing the order we were told by your designers that there would be a provision, no one has updated us on it yet.
 8. One of the almirah that has been installed is not occupying the complete space that was left for it as per our architectural design cause of which that space has been wasted. When your designers visited us they did not tell us that your almirah sizes are standard that we will be wasting the designated storage space if we get livspace almirahs.
 9. The kitchen lights were delivered on site and were not installed till the last moment, infact your team was not even aware of it. They only got to know about it and got them installed when I myself pulled out the lights that were delivered along with other material and informed your team of it.
 10. Your team installed kitchen skirtings without even cleaning the area behind it. We are extremely disappointed with our experience.We chose Livspace trusting the brand and the promise of quality, but the execution has been deeply frustrating and stressful. What was meant to be a 4-month project stretched to 14 months, and we were ultimately forced to move into our home without a proper handover. The quality of work is far below expectation. Despite installing premium fittings like Kohler, the execution was handled poorly. Basic things like toilet installation were done incorrectly, leading to water spilling during use. Balcony drainage was blocked during tiling, forcing us to redo the work at our own cost. Modular cabinets are already coming loose, paint touch-ups were never completed, and existing seepage issues were not addressed. Electrical planning is another major concern, with multiple lights clubbed on single switches, defeating the entire design intent.
 What is most concerning is the complete lack of accountability and supervision. We had to constantly follow up during the project, and even now, several issues remain unresolved.
 Despite writing email and reaching out to their after-sales team for support, there has been no response whatsoever. No acknowledgment, no follow-up, no ownership.
 We have tried to remain patient and respectful throughout this process, but at this stage, it feels important to share this experience so that others can make an informed decision.

I paid ₹25,000 to HomeLane for interior design services on 31st January 2026. I was verbally told this amount is refundable.
What I got: Nothing. No site measurement. No design. No 3D. Zero.
What happened when I asked for refund:
Three different people gave three different excuses:
∙	Jaykant Shukla said it’s a “booking amount”
∙	Himanshu Batra (Branch Manager) said “measurements taken internally”
∙	Sagar Taneja said it’s “non-refundable Lux consultation fee”
The story kept changing.
The truth:
∙	Their own Process Document says: “Before Site Measurement → 100% refund”
∙	Site measurement was never done
∙	Sagar Taneja admitted on WhatsApp: “I accept that sharing Lux brochure was miss” - meaning the terms were never shown before payment
∙	I never signed any document agreeing to non-refundable terms
Current status:
∙	Filed complaint with National Consumer Helpline (Docket: 8651476)
∙	HomeLane has already lost similar cases in Consumer Court
My advice: Don’t pay anything to HomeLane. They take your money, deliver nothing, then make excuses. Same pattern on Trustpilot, MouthShut, and ConsumerComplaints.
I have full WhatsApp screenshots as proof.
Avoid HomeLane.

🚨 Warning: Think Twice Before Choosing HomeLane! 🚨

I feel compelled to share my terrible experience with HomeLane to warn others. Their overcommitment, poor quality, and exorbitant pricing make them a nightmare to deal with.

Overpromising, Under-Delivering– They make grand promises but fail miserably in execution. Their delivery timelines are a joke—you have to chase them endlessly to get things done.

Pathetic Vendor Selection – The vendors they choose are unreliable, never show up on time, and require constant follow-ups. Instead of taking responsibility, HomeLane expects YOU to do the chasing.

Ridiculously Overpriced – The cost of work that can be done at a reasonable rate elsewhere? HomeLane charges 5X the price! For instance, their own vendor is willing to complete our POP work for ₹20K, yet HomeLane demands 5X that amount—and even after such insane margins, the quality is pathetic.

Non-Responsive Customer Support – I have been sending emails to their AGM for over a month, and guess what? ZERO response. They just don’t care once they’ve taken your money. Had to involve their CEO and COO in the email exchange just to get a response—this speaks volumes about their customer service.

⚠️ Don't fall for their flashy ads! The reality is far from what they market. Engage with them at your own risk. If you value your time, money, and peace of mind, stay far away!

#HomeLane #CustomerExperience #BadService #PoorQuality #Overpriced #FalsePromises

"Honest Review: A Journey with Ups and Downs, but a Satisfying End!

We started our home renovation journey with Homelane with high expectations, and initially, everything seemed perfect. Our designer Priya was fantastic, and we loved the initial design concept.

However, as we progressed, we hit some bumps. We had to make some customizations, which led to a few miscommunications and delays. Managing sub-orders also became a bit of a challenge.

But, here's the thing - the Homelane team, especially our project manager Aishwarya and Priya worked diligently to get things back on track.

The end result? We're thrilled with our newly designed home! The quality of work, the attention to detail, and the final finish are all top-notch.

If you're considering Homelane, be prepared for a potentially bumpy ride, but know that they'll work hard to ensure a satisfying end result.

Rating: 4/5 stars"

The 5 stars are for the two people who made a sincere and honest effort in our home renovation journey with Homelane. Sapna Rajput (designer) and Aishwary Goel (project manager) were both prompt and perceptive to our needs and did a very good job with the delivery.
Quick, efficient, perfect. We especially loved how they incorporated our existing furniture pieces into the new design, creating a perfect blend of old and new.

Our experience with HomeLane was a long and bumpy journey right from the start. During our decision-making process between the two big names—Livspace and HomeLane—we were promised great support and a seamless experience. Some very persuasive salespeople convinced us with big promises, which we believed, given HL’s structured process and the price we were paying.

The design phase was where reality hit us. Our meetings stretched for 7-8 hours because, apparently, the client is expected to do most of the design work. We assumed that having a dedicated designer meant they would take the lead, but instead, we found ourselves doing all the research while the designer merely guided us on what would or wouldn’t work. To her credit, she did understand our taste and didn’t impose her own, but the process dragged on for three months—until I escalated the delay and pushed the HL team leads to take action. Overall, the onboarding promises didn’t match the actual experience, and accountability was severely lacking. The team was quick to create a WhatsApp group but often ignored queries, even when asked multiple times.

Once we moved from design to execution, we finally saw progress. Fortunately, we had Ibadat as our project manager—professional, knowledgeable, and detail-oriented. Even for tasks outside HL’s scope, he stepped in with valuable insights and guided the vendors whenever needed. Thanks to him, our modular installation was seamless.

In the end, while our journey started on a frustrating note, Ibadat’s expertise and dedication helped turn things around. That’s the reason for the 4-star rating instead of a 2.

Thank you, Ibadat! For you, the individual rating will be 5/5 :) All the very best for your future projects..
